title: Brand Licensing Europe Exhibitor Numbers at Record High as Visitor Registration Opens
author: Not Specified
description: Brand Licensing Europe (BLE) announces record growth for its October 2026 event at Excel London, with exhibitor numbers up 16% year-on-year and 222 confirmed exhibitors. The event follows a successful 2025 edition that attracted over 11,000 visitors. New exhibitors include Tate Modern, ATP Tour, and Del Monte Europe, while returning participants span heritage, sports, entertainment, and retail sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as returning exhibitor at BLE 2026, indicating continued commitment to European licensing market and confidence in industry growth

Returning exhibitor at growing event, indicating active participation in brand licensing expansion
Confirmed returning exhibitor, demonstrating continued focus on licensing opportunities in European market

title: Stock Market Today, May 20: Nasdaq Gains 1.5% as Risk Appetite Returns
author: Emma Newbery
description: Major U.S. stock indices gained on May 20, 2026, with the Nasdaq rising 1.54%, S&P 500 up 1.08%, and Dow Jones advancing 1.31%. The rally was driven by falling oil prices and easing Treasury yields amid hopes of an end to the U.S.-Iran conflict. AI hardware leaders and software companies recovered ground, while Nvidia climbed ahead of earnings. Hasbro fell 9% despite beating earnings due to cautious guidance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock climbed 1.3% ahead of earnings as an AI bellwether, though initial after-hours reaction was muted due to lower-than-expected sales forecast guidance.
AI hardware leader climbed on renewed confidence in the sector.
AI hardware leader gained alongside AMD on renewed sector confidence.

Cybersecurity name recovered ground as broader market sentiment improved.

title: S&P 500 Rebounds Ahead Of Nvidia's Print, Oil Sinks 5% On Iran Truce Hopes: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stock markets rebounded midday Wednesday as oil prices fell over 5% on hopes of U.S.-Iran de-escalation. The S&P 500 climbed 0.9% to 7,418, with the Russell 2000 outperforming at +2.3%. Semiconductor stocks led gains ahead of Nvidia's earnings, while energy stocks declined. Airlines benefited from lower fuel costs, but energy companies and some earnings reporters faced headwinds.

title: Stock Market: Will S&P 500 Open Up Or Down Today?
author: Eva Mathew
description: The S&P 500 fell 0.67% on Tuesday to 7,353.61 amid rising Treasury yields driven by inflation concerns. However, Polymarket traders predict a 71% probability of a higher open on Wednesday. Key catalysts include Nvidia's Q1 earnings report after market close, Fed meeting minutes, and earnings from Lowe's, Target, Hasbro, Analog Devices, and TJX. S&P 500 futures were up 0.21% in early trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia's Q1 earnings report is highlighted as a major catalyst for market movement. The article notes investors view AI spending and earnings growth as powerful market drivers, and analysts are focused on whether the AI supercycle has years of growth ahead.

title: Evan Shapiro’s Media Universe Summit Returns to Questex’s StreamTV Show 2026 with Expanded Agenda and New Industry Case Studies
author: Not Specified
description: Evan Shapiro's Media Universe Summit returns to StreamTV Show 2026 in Denver (June 16-19) with an expanded agenda focused on the 'Affinity Economy' and what's working in media. The summit will feature keynotes, case studies, and panel discussions on modern media models, animation's future, new KPIs, and media M&A trends, bringing together executives, creators, and analysts to navigate industry disruption.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: YouTube representatives moderating and providing data on animation and content trends; active participation in shaping industry conversation suggests strong market position.
